The Slice to List Visitor — Slicing into a collection of multi-dimensional arrays¶

New in version 0.1.

Module author: Claude Gibert <claude.gibert@synopticview.co.uk>

class slicer.slice2list.Slice2List(object)¶

Methods¶

Slice2List(visitor):
Parameters:visitor – a visitor to handle the creation of arrays.

Slice2List create a discrete list of n dimensional arrays (e.g. a list of vertical slices or a list of horizontal fields) with their associated metadata. The visitor should provided the following interface:

def __call__(self,slices,metadata,**kargs):
    # slices: a list of one slice (exceptionally 2) to extract from the source array
    # metadata: metadata regarding the dimensions of the array
    # kargs: metadata extracted from the original directive passed to the slicer

def preProcess(self,dimensions,source,shape,slices,metadata):
    # see :class:`slicer.slicer.Slicer`

def postProcess(self,dimensions,source,shape,slices,metadata):
    # see :class:`slicer.slicer.Slicer`

def fieldSet(self,new = None):
    # should return a collection of created fields / arrays.
__call__(dimensions,variable,src,dst,meta,is_interval,**kargs):

The parameters are described in slicer.slicer.Slicer. Copies slices of variable into a newly create array.

preProcess(dimensions,variable,shape,slices,metadata):

calls the visitor’s preProcess method.

postProcess(dimensions,variable,shape,slices,metadata):

calls the visitor’s postProcess method.

fieldset():

returns the generated fields.fieldset.FieldSet.
